title: ES Core
text:
U.S. large-cap equity futures as a permanent core with dynamic allocation to eight alternatives across equities, fixed income, real assets, and digital assets.
← Back

title: ES Core
text:
Instrument Universe
The strategy trades seven futures contracts: ES (S&P 500), NQ (Nasdaq-100), RTY (Russell 2000), GC (Gold), SI (Silver), ZN (10-year Treasury), and MBT (Micro Bitcoin). All are USD-denominated and trade on regulated U.S. exchanges.
ES is the permanent core. U.S. large-cap equity has been the most reliably positive long-run risk premium available to investors. Every alternative is evaluated against ES on a risk-adjusted basis. The permanent equity core ensures the strategy participates in the growth regime that historically accounts for the majority of compounded long-run return.

title: ES Core
text:
Strategy Design
ES Core maintains a permanent allocation to ES and dynamically allocates up to 50% of the portfolio to the six alternative instruments based on their risk-adjusted performance relative to ES.
Framework:

Permanent core: ES with hard floor at 50%
Tactical sleeve: inverse downside semi-deviation weighted
Sleeve qualification: rolling Sortino vs ES > 0
Volatility target: 10–28% downside semi-deviation
Leverage: dynamic, max 3.0x
EMA(10) smoothing + 10% no-trade band
Signal lag: 1 day

Leverage is structurally necessary. The volatility targeting mechanism determines how much notional exposure each dollar of capital should support. In high-conviction, low-volatility environments the strategy runs above 1x notional leverage. In uncertain or high-volatility environments it contracts toward or below 1x.

title: SPY Core
text:
← Dog Capital
Monthly vol-targeting accumulation into SPY CEDEARs via Balanz. No leverage. Designed for Argentine investors building USD-denominated savings systematically.

title: SPY Core
text:
How it works
Each month, the strategy computes the realized downside volatility of SPY over the prior 21 trading days. It then sizes the monthly buy to bring total exposure toward a 20% annualized downside volatility target.
When markets are calm, the full monthly allocation is deployed. When realized volatility exceeds the target, the strategy scales back — or holds — and parks idle capital in a dólar-linked money market fund (Balanz Fondo Dólar Linked). No selling unless a sustained volatility breach triggers a REDUCE alert.
Signal logic:

Instrument: SPY (CEDEAR, 10:1 ratio, Balanz)
Volatility measure: downside semi-deviation of daily returns
Lookback: 21 trading days
Vol target: 20% annualized
Leverage: none — strategy can only de-risk, never lever up
Smoothing: EMA(10) + 10% no-trade band to reduce unnecessary turnover
Cadence: monthly execution, first trading day of the month
Idle capital: Balanz Fondo Dólar Linked

The strategy gives up a small amount of raw return in exchange for meaningfully better risk-adjusted performance and a 18 percentage point shallower max drawdown. For a long-term accumulation program, the drawdown reduction matters more than the CAGR gap.
Backtest on USD underlying (SPY), $10,000 lump sum, no contributions.

title: SPY Core
text:
Live deployment
Running live on Balanz since June 2026. Monthly signals sent via Telegram. Live performance will be published here as it accumulates.

title: SPY Core
text:
Why CEDEARs
CEDEARs (Certificados de Depósito Argentinos) are Argentine depositary receipts that track USD-denominated underlying assets like SPY. They trade on BYMA in pesos but are priced at the MEP dollar rate, giving Argentine investors effective USD exposure without needing to move money offshore.
SPY Core operates entirely on local infrastructure — no foreign account, no capital transfer, no AFIP complications. You buy CEDEARs on Balanz, the system tells you how many, you execute.
